behavior_summary: U8P_LAVA::look is the object's 'U8P_LAVA::look' (examine) handler: it presents a short description 'molten rock' to the player and then yields/suspends.

U8P_LAVA::func00AF is a helper that appears to drive the lava/fire visual state and lifecycle: it reads the item's current frame, advances or reverses the frame index based on comparisons with constants, updates the item's frame, uses repeated spawn calls (spawn ->FREE::func1C19) with suspend points (inferred delays/timers), toggles small local control values, and eventually calls Item::destroy(this) when a terminal frame/state is reached.
The helper logic is likely reused by FIRE/PYROS-class objects to sequence animation frames and cleanup; exact numeric thresholds and timing are inferred from constants and spawn/suspend patterns.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LAVA::look: single linear flow — bark 'molten rock' then suspend/return.

U8P_LAVA::func00AF: loop/sequence-like flow driven by frame value: read Item::getFrame(this) into a temporary, compare to constants (0x00, 0x05, 0x08), choose increment/decrement behavior, call Item::setFrame(..., this), then spawn ->FREE::func1C19 and suspend to time the next step.

U8P_LAVA::func00AF sets small local control values (local2) to different constants (examples: 0x07, 0x01, 0x20, 0x2D) to control direction or timing; upon reaching a terminal frame the function eventually invokes Item::destroy(this).
open_questions: What is the precise encoding and semantic role of local2 (is it a bitmask, a direction flag, or a spawn handle)?
What exact delays/timing does spawn ->FREE::func1C19 provide and how long are the suspend intervals in gameplay seconds?
Which external scripts/processes explicitly call U8P_LAVA::func00AF at runtime (metadata mentions FIRE and PYROS classes but the direct callers in-game are not enumerated here)?
What are the exact numeric frame thresholds for animation transitions and destruction (constants like 0x05, 0x08 are observed but mapping to visual frames is inferred)?
